subject: English
source: Aruvi static lookup. Derived from NCF Languages CG (Section 2.4.1.2) and a 6-chapter mapping audit (VII Ch 3, VII Ch 4) showing 80% C-code overlap across chapters. The 6-spine itself carries NCF compliance — per-chapter mapping is redundant.

reading_for_comprehension.label: Reading for Comprehension
reading_for_comprehension.notes: Read-and-summarise, identification of literary forms, and identification of literary devices.

listening.label: Listening
listening.notes: Listening for main points and listening to interviews / structured-unstructured dialogues.

speaking.label: Speaking
speaking.notes: Interviews, probing questions, and oral expression of ideas/critiques.

writing.label: Writing
writing.notes: Letters/essays/reports, audio-visual content scripts, prose/poetry/drama. The chapter's task_type tag (paragraph / report / poem / dialogue / script) selects the dominant code.

vocabulary_grammar.label: Vocabulary and Grammar
vocabulary_grammar.notes: Linguistic aspects (sentence structure, tense, parts of speech), phonetics/script, wordplay, and word games.

beyond_text.label: Beyond the Text
beyond_text.notes: Literary appreciation explorations and use of references for projects/interdisciplinary work.
